title: The loop that never ends
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was. Both loops are infinite.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.frozen: Your code never stops. It looped forever and was cut off.
hint2.frozen: Check both loops. The first never changes the stock. The second changes it, but never lands on zero.
hint3.frozen: 500 minus 30 repeatedly gives 20, then minus 10, then minus 40. Never exactly zero.
hint2.changed-data: The numbers stay. The loops get fixed, not the input.
hint2.wrong-shape: Both stay loops. The task is to make them end, not to remove them.
hint2.wrong-served: The first loop increases the served count but never reduces the stock. The condition stays true forever.
hint3.wrong-served: The three parts of a loop: start, condition, change. The third is missing here — nothing moves towards the end.
why.stock-untouched: The stock has not reached zero.
hint2.stock-untouched: Every cup served should reduce the stock.
hint2.wrong-batches: 500 grams in batches of 30. Seventeen batches, the last one incomplete.
hint3.wrong-batches: The condition for exactly zero never comes true. Check whether beans are left, not whether they ran out exactly.
hint4.wrong-batches: Change the comparison from not equal to greater than.
why.wrong-count: There are not two lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Two loops. One counts customers served while there is stock. The other divides beans into batches.
blocks.1.text: Both are infinite. Submit them as they are and see what happens.
blocks.3.text: The first time it is alarming. The page stops responding, buttons do nothing, the browser asks whether to stop it.
blocks.4.text: This happens to everyone. Including people who have worked with code for twenty years. The difference is that they recognise it in two seconds and know where to look.
blocks.5.text: Here submitting is protected — the code runs separately and is cut off after two seconds. The preview beside it is not, and it really will freeze. Change the code and it comes back.
blocks.7.text: The condition checks the stock. The body increases the served count. The stock is never touched anywhere.
blocks.8.text: The condition is true at the start and stays true forever, because nothing changes the thing being checked.
blocks.9.quote: Check one thing and change another, and the loop can never end.
blocks.10.text: The second kind: it moves, but overshoots
blocks.11.text: Here something does change. It simply never lands on the value being checked.
blocks.13.text: 500 minus 30 gets down to 20. Then 20 minus 30 gives minus 10. Then minus 40. The number walks straight past zero and carries on down forever.
blocks.14.text: This kind is nastier, because with other numbers it works. Put 480 instead of 500 and the loop ends beautifully. The code is wrong either way — it just does not show in one of them.
blocks.16.text: Make the exit condition a range, not a point. Do not ask whether it hit exactly zero, ask whether anything is left.
blocks.17.items.0: greater than or less than, instead of equal and not equal
blocks.17.items.1: and check that something in the condition really changes inside the body
